title: Protocol for assembling SARS-Cov-2 runs  with s-aligner
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"amjfu","text":"This is a protocol for using Contignant s-aligner to de-novo assemble SARS-Cov-2 genomes. S-aligner outperforms common open-source software for de novo-assembly of viruses by 110% increased performance.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Index the reads in the run. You can use as input a FASTA file (preferred) or a FASTQ. The file can also be compressed with gzip, having a .gz extension. The script will uncompress the file and pass it to FASTA format if it's a FASTQ file. ./index.sh your_run_id /mnt/c/your_run.fastq.gz

Assemble ./assemble.sh your-run-id -output alignments 500 > results/your-run-id-500.fa Assemble up to processing 500 reads results/your- run- id-500.fa should have the largest contig bigger than 1.200bp and at least one of the files containing aligned reads to form a contig should look like that:
Map your reads to a reference genome to see what's going wrong. ./map.sh your-run-id sequences/your-run-id/sra_data.part-71.fa reference.fa 4000 > results/map-your-run-id.fa
Trim your data and start again. go to step #1

You should see something like that. Copy the session id to the clipboard. Continue assembling ./assemble.sh your-run-id -sid 61D90743D16026D7 3500 > results/your-run-id-3500.fa You should paste the session id from the clipboard.
go to step #2 and continue as if the contig length is >1.200bp
Discard this dataset or look for some tool that can filter your data.
go to step #1 but index a larger fraction of the dataset. ./index.sh your_run_id /mnt/c/your_run.fastq.gz -n 350000
go to step #3 increasing the number of reads until you get a satisfactory assembly. ./assemble.sh your-run-id -sid 61D90743D16026D7 6000 > results/your-run-id-6000.fa
Use QUAST to get statistics. quast.py -R reference_file.fasta results/your-run-id-6000.fa
Try scaffolding the contigs ./scaffold.sh results/your-run-id-xxxx.fa Then use QUAST with the result quast.py -R reference_file.fasta results/your-run-id-xxxx.fa
creator: Juanjo Bermúdez
